name: User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess'
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Roberto Rodriguez (source), Ilyas Ochkov (rule), oscd.community
SIGMA Title: User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess'
SIGMA Description: The 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess' function verifies that the application making the function call is a logon process by checking that it has the SeTcbPrivilege privilege set. Possible Rubeus tries to get a handle to LSA.

False positives:
- Unknown

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4673 AND "Service Name"='LsaRegisterLogonProcess()' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%0x8010000000000000%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess' on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4673 AND "Service Name"='LsaRegisterLogonProcess()' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%0x8010000000000000%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess'
